layers.title: Sampling wire schema
layers.summary: The protocol layer replaces the old scalar log limit with a structured sampling configuration and extends heartbeat responses to carry the same configuration. This is the compatibility root for downstream client and RPC behavior because field 24 keeps its number but changes type and meaning.

layers.title: Sampler replaces limiter
layers.summary: On top of the schema, the runtime logging path removes the first-N per-second limiter and adds LogSampler snapshots, ppm thresholds, categorized runtime decisions, and proto serialization. The hot path shifts from rate-window atomics to a fast enabled check plus cached trace decisions, while shutdown now owns sampler snapshot cleanup.

layers.title: Configuration APIs update
layers.summary: After the sampler exists, public and dynamic configuration move from one integer limit to three double sample rates with range validation and transactional updates. Embedded workers now expose explicit typed setters for request, access, and diagnostic sampling instead of LogRateLimit.

layers.title: RPC state propagation
layers.summary: The worker now populates LogSampleConfigPb during register and heartbeat, and client/ZMQ code applies sampler configuration instead of setting a local rate limiter. This layer binds worker-side sampling policy to clients so one request keeps consistent decisions across process boundaries.

bcFindings.evidence: include/datasystem/utils/embedded_config.h removes `EmbeddedConfig &LogRateLimit(int limit);` and replaces it with `RequestSampleRate(double)`, `AccessSampleRate(double)`, and `DiagnosticSampleRate(double)`.
src/datasystem/protos/share_memory.proto changes field 24 from `int32 log_rate_limit = 24;` to `LogSampleConfigPb log_sample_config = 24;`, changing the wire field type and generated accessors.

src/datasystem/common/log/spdlog/log_rate_limiter.h and .cpp are deleted, removing the `LogRateLimiter` class and its public methods such as `Instance`, `ShouldLog`, `SetRate`, and `GetOrCreateRequestDecision`.

layers.title: Recorder facade redesign
layers.summary: This layer changes AccessRecorder from eager RequestParam construction to typed object, stream, and request-out facades guarded by sampler decisions. Data size becomes numeric and expensive field providers are only evaluated when the record is sampled in.

layers.title: Client callsites migrate
layers.summary: With the facade in place, C, C++, Java, and Python binding call sites stop building request parameters before they know logging will happen. The same access metadata is now filled through chained setters or lazy providers on object and stream client paths.

layers.title: Worker callsites migrate
layers.summary: Worker object-cache, stream-cache, ETCD, metrics, and request-table paths are then moved to the same lazy recorder types. This keeps the foreground request paths from doing string formatting and vector joining when sampling drops the record.

bcFindings.symbol: AccessRecorder::Record(StatusCode, const std::string &, const RequestParam &, const std::string &)
bcFindings.evidence: src/datasystem/common/log/access_recorder.h removes the eager object Record overload taking string `dataSize` and RequestParam, replacing it with typed recorder factories and `ObjectAccessRecorder::Record()`.

bcFindings.symbol: AccessRecorder::Record(int, const std::string &, const RequestParam &, const std::string &)
bcFindings.evidence: src/datasystem/common/log/access_recorder.h removes the int-code eager Record overload and migrates callers to `Result(...).DataSize(...).Record()` on ObjectAccessRecorder.

src/datasystem/common/log/access_recorder.h removes the stream Record overload taking StreamRequestParam and StreamResponseParam, replacing stream call sites with `AccessRecorder::Stream(...).<fields>.Result(...).Record()`.

layers.title: Design context added
layers.summary: The repo context adds a logging design entry for LogSampler and routes future logging work to it. This gives reviewers the invariants behind the code change, including categories, propagation, and performance expectations.
layers.diff:
+| understand LogSampler design, sampling categories, propagation, or migration from log_rate_limit | `modules/infra/logging/log-sampler-design.md` | `src/datasystem/common/log/log_sampler.*`, `src/datasystem/common/log/access_recorder.*` |
+## LogSampler
+
+`log-sampler-design.md` records the unified random sampling design, public configuration surface, propagation rules, and verification scenarios.

layers.title: Configuration docs updated
layers.summary: User-facing documentation removes `DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT` and replaces `log_rate_limit` examples with request, access, and diagnostic sample rates. This aligns embedded, dscli, and logging guides with the new three-dimensional sampler configuration.

layers.title: Deployment defaults updated
layers.summary: Operational config templates and Helm values stop emitting the removed integer flag and add the three sample-rate knobs. The chart and worker config layers now need to preserve double-valued ranges and explicitness semantics for derived defaults.

layers.title: Unit coverage replaced
layers.summary: The unit-test layer deletes the LogRateLimiter suite and adds focused sampler, access, config, integration, facade, logging, and RPC metadata tests. Coverage checks distribution, derived configuration, explicit overrides, fatal bypass, concurrency, lazy access field construction, and macro behavior.
layers.diff:
+ds_cc_test(
+    name = "log_sampler_test",
+    srcs = ["log_sampler_test.cpp"],
+)
+ds_cc_test(
+    name = "log_sampler_integration_test",
+    srcs = ["log_sampler_integration_test.cpp"],
+)
-    name = "log_rate_limiter_test",
-    srcs = ["log_rate_limiter_test.cpp"],
+TEST_F(LogSamplerTest, ConcurrentConfigUpdateAndHotPathRead)
+TEST_F(LogSamplerAccessTest, AccessGuardSkipsConstructionWhenDropped)
+TEST_F(LoggingTest, TestAccessLogSampledMarker)

layers.title: Performance tests refocused
layers.summary: Performance coverage is updated from rate-limit quotas to sampler pass-through, reject-fast-path, mixed sampling, access recorder sampled-out, and sampler-only decision cost scenarios. This directly exercises the claimed hot-path savings from lazy access construction and random threshold sampling.

layers.title: System propagation covered
layers.summary: System-test coverage verifies that worker-delivered sampler config reaches clients and that cross-process request sampling stays consistent. The existing object-cache log-size test is adjusted to use sample-rate flags instead of the removed rate-limit flag.
layers.diff:
+ds_cc_test(
+    name = "log_sampler_st_test",
+    srcs = ["log_sampler_st_test.cpp"],
+    tags = ["manual"],
+)
+TEST_F(LogSamplerSTTest, CrossProcessSamplingConsistency)
+TEST_F(LogSamplerSTTest, WorkerConfigDeliveryViaRegister)
-        opts.workerGflagParams = "-shared_memory_size_mb=128 -v=2 -log_monitor=true -log_rate_limit=0";
+        opts.workerGflagParams = "-shared_memory_size_mb=128 -v=2 -log_monitor=true -request_sample_rate=1.0";

summary: This PR replaces integer log rate limiting with unified random log sampling, propagates sampler config and decisions across clients and workers, migrates access logging to lazy sampled-in construction, and updates deployment docs plus regression coverage.
